meeting.title: AI Meeting Intelligence Platform

transcript.speaker: Aarav
transcript.text: Alright, let’s get started. The objective today is simple—lock down MVP scope. No overengineering. We need something demo-ready in 2 weeks.

transcript.speaker: Meera
transcript.text: From a business perspective, the core value is capturing meeting decisions and context. If we miss that, the product is useless.

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: Then we need to define what decision means technically. Are we extracting action items, summaries, or explicit decisions?

transcript.speaker: Riya
transcript.text: Detecting decisions is non-trivial. We can start with action item extraction, key summary generation, and speaker tagging.

transcript.speaker: Aarav
transcript.text: So MVP equals summaries plus action items. Drop decision intelligence for now?

transcript.speaker: Meera
transcript.text: Yes, but messaging should still say decision support, not decision detection.

transcript.speaker: Karan
transcript.text: UI-wise, I’ll need a transcript panel, highlighted action items, and timeline navigation. Do we want real-time or post-meeting processing?

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: Real-time is risky due to latency and infrastructure complexity. We should process after the meeting ends.

transcript.speaker: Riya
transcript.text: Agree. Real-time NLP will break under noise and overlapping speech.

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: Tech stack proposal: FastAPI backend, Redis queue, PostgreSQL storage, Whisper for transcription.

transcript.speaker: Riya
transcript.text: Whisper is fine, but we should use a smaller model for speed.

transcript.speaker: Aarav
transcript.text: Speed over perfection for MVP. Users tolerate minor errors, not delays.

transcript.speaker: Karan
transcript.text: API response format? I need structured JSON.

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: You’ll get summary, action_items, and speakers in structured format.

transcript.speaker: Riya
transcript.text: We should also store embeddings for future search.

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: That’s scope creep. Not needed for MVP.

transcript.speaker: Meera
transcript.text: Key risks include accuracy complaints, slow processing, and poor UI clarity.

transcript.speaker: Nayan
transcript.text: Biggest risk is infrastructure scaling if usage spikes.

transcript.speaker: Aarav
transcript.text: Final decisions: MVP includes summary and action items, post-meeting processing only, simple architecture. Deadline is 2 weeks.

decisions: MVP will include summary and action item extraction
Post-meeting processing will be used instead of real-time
Avoid advanced features like embeddings in MVP
